other_uses: The giant columnar cactus of the Sonoran Desert; its fruit harvest opens the Tohono O'odham new year.
prep: Knock down the ripe red fruit with a long rib pole; eat fresh, boil into syrup and jam, ferment into ceremonial wine, and grind the seeds. A protected keystone — harvest only by permission/tradition.
soil: Dry, rocky, sharply drained desert soils.
risks: Protected species; spines. Harvest only where legal/permitted.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
